response.justification: Bogdan is a versatile, senior generalist with genuine developer-tooling credentials — he shipped components for NI LabWindows/CVI (a C IDE), built test runners and assertion libraries, and recently works on MCP code generators — but the role's core requirements are essentially absent. There is no demonstrated Kotlin, no Spring or enterprise Java framework experience, and Java appears only marginally in a career dominated by TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and PHP. Deep JVM expertise is the non-negotiable center of this position, and this resume does not evidence it.
strengths: Real IDE and language-tooling experience: C/C++ components for NI LabWindows/CVI plus self-built test runners, assertion libraries, and MCP code generators
Proven work on complex, large-scale systems (low-latency microservices handling millions of HTTP requests per second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Strong engineering craftsmanship culture: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and a long record of teaching and community workshops
concerns: No Kotlin experience and only incidental Java usage; deep JVM understanding is not evidenced anywhere in the resume
No exposure to Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or the enterprise Java ecosystem the role is built around
Recent focus is TypeScript/Node.js full-stack product work, a significant distance from enterprise JVM IDE subsystems

key_factors.factor: Track record on complex, high-scale systems
